Modify Recursive Call
When an option does not exist and the stopAtNonOption flag is set, the function should add the remaining characters as a single token to the list of processed tokens, rather than making a recursive call. This can be achieved by changing the line `process(token.substring(i));` to `tokens.add("--" + token.substring(i));`. This modification ensures that the remaining characters are treated as a single argument.

Correct Handling of Non-Option Characters
When an option does not exist and the stopAtNonOption flag is not set, the function should add the current character prepended with '-' to the list of tokens, rather than adding the entire token. This can be achieved by changing the line `tokens.add(token);` to `tokens.add("-" + ch);`. This modification ensures that the current character is correctly handled as a non-option character.

Improve Token Bursting Logic
The function should be modified to correctly handle the bursting of tokens when an option has an argument value. This can be achieved by adding a conditional statement to check if the current option has an argument value, and if so, add the remaining characters as a token to the list of processed tokens. The modified code would look like this: `if (currentOption.hasArg() && (token.length() != (i + 1))) { tokens.add(token.substring(i + 1)); break; }`. This modification ensures that the token bursting logic is correct and handles options with argument values properly.
